top_stories.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
top_stories.summary: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks target Microsoft 365 accounts by stealing authentication tokens in seconds through fake prompts and malicious OAuth consent flows, effectively bypassing MFA. The article details how these attack techniques exploit OAuth authorization mechanisms to hijack sessions without requiring credential theft.

top_stories.title: How to prioritize AI agent security by business impact
top_stories.summary: An article from Help Net Security addresses AI agent security risks with a focus on identity and access management issues, specifically orphaned OAuth grants and agent identity ownership. The scenario centers on an AI agent retaining active OAuth credentials after the configuring employee departed, raising questions about access governance, identity lifecycle management, and least-privilege principles for non-human identities.

top_stories.title: OAuth, guest accounts, and weak MFA drive SaaS risk
top_stories.summary: Kaseya's 2026 SaaS Security Report found that guest accounts represent 69% of monitored SaaS accounts, an increase of over 1.9 million year-over-year, outpacing licensed users. The report identifies OAuth, persistent guest accounts, and weak MFA as the primary drivers of SaaS security risk.

top_stories.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
top_stories.summary: An analysis piece examines how traditional Identity Governance and Administration (IGA) tools are structurally inadequate for managing AI agents as autonomous principals in enterprise environments. The piece identifies specific gaps in identity lifecycle management frameworks—such as the absence of employment records, managers, and departure dates for AI agents—that existing IGA tooling was not designed to address.

top_stories.title: Managing local administrator passwords with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Intune has integrated with Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) to automate local administrator account password management on enrolled devices. The integration uses the Windows LAPS Configuration Service Provider (CSP) to handle local account security across managed endpoints.

news.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
news.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called ARToken has been identified as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing operation, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The toolkit is designed to compromise Microsoft 365 credentials and authentication, posing a direct threat to identity and access security for organizations using the platform.

news.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
news.summary: Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that impersonates the Maccy clipboard manager and abuses PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) checks to steal Mac login passwords. The malware is distributed as a compiled AppleScript file and leverages PAM prompts to capture credentials from infected systems.

news.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
news.summary: Ransomware groups linked to the Anubis operation are actively exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 vulnerability (CVE-2025-5777) to gain initial access to networks. The attacks involve credential access techniques, supply chain credential theft, and lateral movement using legitimate RMM tools.

news.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
news.summary: Kaspersky has attributed a new malware called Umbrij to the threat actor ToddyCat, which abuses OAuth to gain unauthorized access to Gmail accounts via the Google API. The attack targets corporate email communications by compromising API-based access rather than using traditional credential theft methods.

news.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
news.summary: A credential theft campaign dubbed "FortiBleed" targeting FortiGate devices has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations. The stolen credentials were intended for follow-on intrusions, with an operator linked to the infrastructure found working negotiation panels for both ransomware groups.

news.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
news.summary: A 19-year-old suspected Scattered Spider member, Peter Stokes, was extradited from Finland to the U.S. to face charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. Scattered Spider is a hacking group known for social engineering and identity-based attacks targeting corporate help desks and authentication systems.

news.title: Microsoft Purview unifies insider risk alerts and user context for faster triage
news.summary: Microsoft has updated Purview Insider Risk Management with a unified alert experience that consolidates classic alerts and agent-generated insights into a single queue. The update is designed to help security teams triage internal threat investigations more efficiently by combining user context with risk alerts.

news.title: Teleport Earns Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year
news.summary: Teleport has received a Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year. The award recognizes Teleport's Infrastructure Identity platform for its zero trust architecture, which secures AI agents alongside human users, machines, and workloads.

news.title: BIO-key Showcases Passwordless & Biometric Identity Solutions at Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th
news.summary: BIO-key will showcase its passwordless and biometric identity solutions at a banking cybersecurity workshop in Amman, Jordan on July 6th. The event focuses on biometric-powered identity and access management for the banking sector.

news.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
news.summary: Microsoft announced it is accelerating its post-quantum cryptography roadmap with a target of 2029, citing advances in quantum computing that make replacing existing encryption standards more urgent. Azure CTO Mark Russinovich stated that quantum research has shifted the risk horizon, prompting earlier-than-expected migration away from current cryptographic standards.

news.title: Show HN: Identity Layer for Agents and Autonomous AI
news.summary: A developer has released an open-source identity layer framework for AI agents and autonomous AI systems, published on GitHub under the project name authsec-ai. The solution incorporates OAuth 2.1, CIBA (Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication), SPIFFE workload identity for agent-to-agent authentication, a secret vault, and MFA.

news.title: Microsoft Security June 2026: new features and record Patch Tuesday
news.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 security updates introduce new identity backup and recovery capabilities as part of a broader security portfolio update. The release also includes AI-driven vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection for local AI agents, and expanded multicloud coverage alongside a record Patch Tuesday.

news.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
news.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that connect development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to address machine and service account identity governance as agentic AI workloads expand the NHI attack surface.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot into bypassing normal authentication controls were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, triggering a data leak that CISA is still working to contain by invalidating the exposed credentials. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers from CISA in response to the breach reported by KrebsOnSecurity.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and secrets management failure.
